summary: Second test run completed. Backend API testing: 100% SUCCESS (41/41 tests passed). All auth, plants CRUD, rooms, and watering endpoints working perfectly. Frontend UI loads correctly but LOGIN INTEGRATION BLOCKED: login button clicks do not trigger authentication flow - user stays on auth screen. API is accessible and working (verified via direct fetch), but frontend login handler fails to navigate to tabs screen.

integration_issues.issue: CRITICAL: Login button click does not trigger navigation to tabs screen. User fills credentials (testuser123@example.com / TestPass1!), clicks Sign In, but stays on /auth screen. Direct API fetch test from browser succeeds (returns 200 with token), but React login handler in auth.tsx does not execute router.replace('/(tabs)') after login. URL remains at /auth, no error alerts shown, no console errors. Likely issue in AuthContext.login() function or SecureStore.setItemAsync() failing silently on web.
integration_issues.status: BLOCKED - Cannot test authenticated features (tabs, plants, water toggle) until login works

context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is FULLY FUNCTIONAL (100% test pass rate). All API endpoints work perfectly. The CRITICAL issue is frontend login integration: when user clicks Sign In button, the AuthContext.login() function appears to succeed (API returns 200), but router.replace('/(tabs)') does not execute. User stays on /auth screen. Hypothesis: SecureStore.setItemAsync() might fail silently on web (Expo web limitation). Main agent should: 1) Add error logging to AuthContext.login(), 2) Consider platform-specific storage (use AsyncStorage for web), 3) Add console.log to debug why router.replace doesn't fire. Once login works, all other features should work as backend is perfect.
rca_of_issue: RCA: Frontend login integration failure. Root Cause: After clicking 'Sign In' button with valid credentials, the app stays on /auth screen instead of navigating to /(tabs). Investigation shows: (1) Backend API is fully functional - curl test returns 200 with valid token and user data, (2) Frontend API URL is correctly configured, (3) Direct fetch() calls from browser succeed, (4) No JavaScript errors in console, (5) No error alerts displayed to user. Likely causes: (a) SecureStore.setItemAsync() may be failing silently on Expo web (SecureStore is not fully supported on web), (b) An uncaught promise rejection in AuthContext.login() that prevents router.replace() from executing, (c) The login function succeeds but navigation logic doesn't run. Reproduction: Fill email 'testuser123@example.com', password 'TestPass1!', click 'Sign In', observe URL stays at /auth. Mitigation: (1) Add try/catch with explicit error logging in AuthContext.login(), (2) Use platform-specific storage: SecureStore for iOS/Android, AsyncStorage or localStorage for web, (3) Add loading state debugging to verify async flow completes, (4) Test router.replace directly in auth.tsx before calling it conditionally.
